title: Cosmic Resonance
definition: The idea that consciousness arises from organized, complex, and self-referential vibrations in the universe.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The idea that our awareness might be a result of certain patterns or frequencies in the universe, similar to how a tuning fork produces a specific sound when struck.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The theory that consciousness is an emergent property of self-referential and organized vibrations within the universe, such as Earth's stable environment, liquid water, and feedback loops in life and nature.
difficulty_levels.expert: A philosophical hypothesis suggesting that consciousness emerges when vibrational systems become sufficiently complex and self-referential, bridging physics, philosophy, and cosmology. The universe, through certain conditions, can give rise to forms of awareness capable of perceiving and questioning its own nature.

extra_fields.insight: The universe might be observing and understanding itself through the emergence of consciousness in certain complex systems, such as Earth and life on it.
